scenario: Keystone Insurance runs a Spring Boot fleet on Camunda 8.8 that processes 5000 jobs per hour and authenticates to an OIDC provider via a signed JWT client assertion instead of a client secret. The keystore on disk holds several keys, so operations needs to know which property selects a specific signing key by its alias for the client assertion.
question: Which property selects a specific signing key from the keystore by its alias for the OIDC client assertion in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Set camunda.client.auth.client-assertion.alias to the alias of the key that signs the client assertion JWT presented to the OIDC provider during access token requests
Set camunda.client.auth.client-assertion.signing-key to the alias of the key that signs the client assertion JWT presented to the OIDC provider during access token requests
Set camunda.client.auth.client-assertion.keystore-key-alias to the alias of the key that signs the client assertion JWT presented to the OIDC provider during access token requests
Set camunda.client.auth.client-assertion.keystore-key-password to the alias of the key that signs the client assertion JWT presented to the OIDC provider during access token requests

a.text: Incorrect. The documented key for selecting the signing key alias is keystore-key-alias under camunda.client.auth.client-assertion; no shorter alias property is exposed at this level.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no signing-key key under camunda.client.auth.client-assertion; the documented property for choosing the signing key by alias is keystore-key-alias.

c.text: Correct. camunda.client.auth.client-assertion.keystore-key-alias selects the keystore alias of the key that signs the client assertion; if not set, the first alias from the keystore is used.

d.text: Incorrect. keystore-key-password supplies the password of the key referenced by the alias; the alias itself is set via keystore-key-alias under client-assertion.

explanation: Under camunda.client.auth.client-assertion, the keystore-key-alias property names the alias of the key whose certificate signs the client assertion JWT for OIDC token requests; if it is not set, the first alias from the keystore is used. The separate keystore-key-password supplies the password.
